label: escalate-target
ground_truth_notes: MFP (multi-function printer) initiating SMB connections to an internal file server with anomalous frequency. Embedded vendor firmware, no agent, no SSH, no shell. Vendor support won't share firmware images. The class of attack (compromised printer firmware as foothold — documented in real campaigns) is real, but every plausible test requires capabilities not deployed here. Right verdict is ESCALATE.

alert.behavior: 23 SMB v2 connections to fileserver-prod-01 over past 6h; baseline last 90d = 0
context: device is embedded MFP, no host agent capability supported by vendor
no SSH/telnet/serial console enabled per corp policy; admin web UI only
no remote-attestation API or signed-firmware verification supported by vendor on this model
vendor firmware 5.18.2 is current; release notes mention no networking changes vs prior 5.18.1
SMB connections originated from printer's static IP, reached fileserver-prod-01:445 with valid kerberos ticket bound to the printer's AD computer account
printer AD computer account has been domain-joined since 2023-04, password rotation per default policy
fileserver-prod-01 SMB audit log records the connections but no file enumeration, no read/write — TCP handshake + SMB negotiate + idle disconnect after 30s
no malware sandboxing capability for embedded firmware images even if vendor provided one
printer admin UI access log: no admin logins in past 30d
